author: doll-agent
content:
Final-deferral phase landed:

(1) Implicit unit promotion in arithmetic
- TimeUnit::ns_per_tick(), TimeUnit::finer(other), TimeUnit::scale_to(target) helpers in ferray-core/src/dtype/datetime.rs.
- ferray-ufunc::ops::datetime promoted variants:
  - sub_datetime_promoted(a, unit_a, b, unit_b) -> (Array<Timedelta64>, TimeUnit)
  - add_datetime_timedelta_promoted
  - add_timedelta_promoted
- Each rescales operands to the finer unit, propagates NaT through rescaling, returns (result, target_unit). 6 unit tests cover same-unit pass-through, mixed Ns/Us, S/Ms, NaT propagation through rescale.

(2) Non-UTC chrono bridges
- DateTime64::from_naive_date / to_naive_date — calendar dates at UTC midnight.
- DateTime64::from_naive_datetime / to_naive_datetime — TZ-naive instants treated as UTC.
- DateTime64::from_fixed_offset / to_fixed_offset — TZ-aware instants converted via UTC; the underlying storage is always UTC (matching NumPy's TZ-naive datetime64). 4 unit tests cover NaiveDate, NaiveDateTime, FixedOffset round-trip preserving the absolute instant, and NaT-returns-None on all variants.

(3) Add/Sub operator overloads
- Scalar: DateTime64 - DateTime64 → Timedelta64; DateTime64 ± Timedelta64 → DateTime64; Timedelta64 ± Timedelta64; -Timedelta64. All in ferray-core/src/dtype/datetime.rs with NaT propagation. 4 unit tests.
- Array-level: &Array<DateTime64, D> - &Array<DateTime64, D> -> FerrayResult<Array<Timedelta64, D>>; &Array<DateTime64, D> ± &Array<Timedelta64, D>. The Timedelta64 + Timedelta64 / Timedelta64 - Timedelta64 array forms are picked up by the existing impl_binary_op! blanket in ferray-core/src/ops.rs (which dispatches through the scalar Add/Sub<Output=Self> impls). 3 unit tests cover the operator-syntax flow end to end.

Verification:
- cargo test --workspace --features ferray/full --features ferray/chrono: 60 test blocks, 0 failures (ferray-core 638 tests).
- cargo clippy --workspace --features ferray/full --features ferray/chrono -- -D warnings: clean.
- cargo fmt: applied.

#343 is now feature-complete: datetime/timedelta dtype is first-class across DType, NPY round-trip, DynArray, scalar+array arithmetic with unit promotion, NaT propagation, chrono interop (UTC/Naive/FixedOffset), Arrow Timestamp/Duration, and operator overloads. Closing.
